author: paulinfrance
text: Hi, got my Aprilia RSV4R last week and it´s the biaggi replique, the French version 78 kw (106hp) its really got a lot of ´Grunt´ especially after my Yamaha R6, but it´s power ´peters´ out at 10.000 rpm, and it drinks petrol, 120/130 km and i am on the reserve ! it takes 14.5 litres to top up,,,,, it´s really light in bends and a delight to ride the only drawbacks are, 1,consumption 2, the saddle, i slip about and it isn´t comfortable 3, No where to put my ´bits´ (wallet, phone and padlock). bye Paul.

author: paulinfrance
text: Tried it out on Full power last week end at the circuit Paul Ricard in the south of France,,,,,, Whaaaaaaaaaa a real beast clocked 237 km h in the rain with the Pirelli track/road tyres not for the faint hearted and Only, 10.8 litres to do 100 km,,, over the double of me XJ6s, the power is much more Linea, easier to ride smoother between 3/4.000 rpm, on the ´road´ setting.

author: mtnbikerva1
text: Update BMW Motorrad Pride in work and Customer Service? 1 Leaking engine oil from a valve cover gasket. not caught or fixed during first service. 2 Pulsating/stiff brake lever or front brake at a crawl speed wise. 3 Backfiring. Related to the gasket leak? 4 Fuel gauge not registering properly even after the recall fix. I ran out of gas on a very dangerous bridge with no cell phone. So again BMW wanted me to run out of gas and maybe get killed before they fix a known defect!!! 5 One of only two screws falling out of headlight mount. And Battley´s wanted me to pay for it!! The moto was about 2 weeks old. 6 Gear display going blank. 7 Missed shifts (could be me I admit, but I wonder) I want to be reasonable, but it seems like a lot of issues/problems. I do not like the fact that I spent $$$$ on a supposedly new motorcycle and now I have to give up my days off/rest to deal with all these problems! Thank you for your time/wisdom and opinions. 8 A BMW dealer called me in I think november and said they had the new airbox in. They had not told me they had ordered it. so it was again another BMW lack of being open and forth coming communication, I think they broke my old one and needed to replace it. So again I probably had to waste another day at the dealership getting it installed. I called BMW Motorrad and asked about it and they were supposed to get back to me about what was going on and if I needed to waste another day at the dealership or if it had been replaced in one of my previous visits by another dealer. Again I have asked and still not received any definitive answer as to whether or not the airbox had been replaced or not. It is now Jan. 15 2010 and no answer. I do not understand why it is so hard to answer what seems like a simple straight forward question. Should I ask KTM or Suzuki questions about BMW motorcycles, service and parts/accessories because BMW to this point has not proven they are capable of a knowledgeable professional answer about their own business, and that is if they even provide any answer at all. The other irritating thing about BMW is they tell the customer to ask the dealers technical or mechanical questions but every person I have asked at each of the 3 dealers and 2or 3 at BMW Motorrad tell me something different about what motor oil to use in the motorcycle. I have been told to switch by mechanics and parts people at the dealers to synthetic motor oil at 3,000 miles, 5k, 6k 7k 10k, 12k, 13k, and maybe 20k( I may be missing some) and one sales person at Bob´s BMW told me never to use synthetic motor oil. One person at BMW Motorrad after I told him what I had been told by all the previous people both at Motorrad and the motorcycle sales person at Bob´s said to never use synthetic. Also one person at Motorrad said to use 20w-50 conventional motor oil. The manual says: Engine oil, capacity 2.9 l, with filter change Lubricant Engine oil, 10W-40 Oil grades Mineral oils of API classification SF through SH. BMW Motorrad recommends not using oil addit- ives, because they can have a detrimental effect on clutch operation. Please do not hesitate to contact your authorised BMW Motorrad dealer if you have any questions relating the choice of a suitable engine oil for your motorcycle. Permissible viscosity classes SAE 10 W-40 L-20 °C, Operation at low temperatures SAE 15 W-40 L-10 °C So since my last post some things have happened. The bike would not run while trying to ride in the rain to work. I had to take the day off. I made BMW flatbead it to Bob´s BMW and they supposedly fixed the factory known defect. Why did BMW not fix this before I bought it and make me go through the hassle and $? I asked for my money back or a new 2010 that hopefully has the bugs worked out, and they say no. I also asked since they will not buy the problem back or swap for a 2010 that I thought it reasonable to pick the motorcycle up and return it to me when fixed, every time the moto had a problem for about a year. BMW Motorrad said no. I have wasted far too many days at the BMW dealership trying to get know defects and other problems fixed. It has also cost me money and stress due to days I had to take off from work. Now BMW Motorrad has said no to my request and instead offered me a \

author: Lee
text: In my opinion: I ride the 2012 BMW 1600 GTL Pro: The bike is a shark, and always exhilarating to ride! Cons: 1st: When the windscreen is fully extended in the up-right position it seemed to create an unusually strong vacuum that pull the rider towards the dash that strains the back after a very short period of time. Con: 2nd The throttle, fly by wire, not sure I’m a total fan yet…..the bike tends to surge @ 40 MPH when in 3rd or 4th gear. Not completely smooth. When you’re on the gas hard, things are great. When you decelerate and downshift, things are also great. But when you try to idle a long at a mellow cruising speed it tends to surge a lot…really annoying aspect for me. Pro: I can’t stop smiling so hard after riding it, it makes my face hurt!

author: Mc.taio
text: Dear All , Having a problem on my r Nine t 2015 warning triangle light appears suddenly , any suggestions . Thanks .

As a Cafe Racer dont expect too much comfort, stock 2 hours in the sale is enough. Ive fitted an Air Hawk and Lambs wool cover to the seat. Ive also fitted riser bars to replace the standard clip-ons. Way better not pressure on the 70 year old wrists. Ive just ridden 912 Klm (570 miles) in one 11 hour day minor butt discomfort,,, so ok. Easy bike to ride goes exactly where its pointed and a nice torque band from idle makes the twisties a joy. Good seat height and not too heavy to manoeuvre. Nice feel to the brakes and suspension is ok for its intended task. No luggage capacity so I use a magnetic tank bag and a back pack for overnight stays. BMW should fit a centre stand, fuel gauge and a quick shifter apart from that, she´s a keeper thoroughly enjoyable.

author: Scottie
text: Ok so I have one (vested interest) and I love it (prejustice) but it´s the sexiest bike I´ve ever owned and that´s NOT just my opinion. I´d had a few bikes from a Meriden Bonneville through XT 500/600´s, a YDS7 and Several 4 cylinder Suzuki´s plus Two Triumph 1200´s and a FJ1200.  That´s before I rediscovered Harley in 2003 (I had a bad experience with an Aermachi back in ´76)  I´ve done the BMW R1200RT thing too, ridden a few sports bikes and travelled overland From the UK to Malaysia. So I do know what I like. Anyway back to the Breakout.  It´s as fast as I need it to be, keep my license and be alert for Road & License Hazards.  It´ll cruise at 120kph and more when allowed, all day long.  It´d ride Hwy 99 at 100mph, IF I was allowed to of course.  It´ll take most curves without any drama if you´re prepared to slide your butt out of the saddle every once in a while to make up for the low ground clearance.  I can ride it all day and comfortably cover 500-700 miles a day for several days straight.  It´s VERY rare for any bike to pass me and even then I can keep up with all but the truly insane until they give up.  An iron butt run wouldn´t bother me a bit, apart from the why.  I´ve toured it from Vancouver in Canada to Death Valley via Denver CO and back to Vancouver BC without missing a beat.  It´s not left me at the side of the road once in 30,000 km.  I looks like a mild chop with it´s raked forks and a massive back tyre but still handles (remember the ground clearance though and look for compression points on fast bends).  Don´t focus on the apex, that´s for the track and this is certainly NOT a track bike!  Watch the vanishing point instead and if you don´t know what that is then just ride slower until you work it out. It has a 5 Gallon tank (unlike the current model) and that takes me past 200 kms, more if I´m in Rubberneck mode.  Less obviously if I´m ripping up the Duffey Lake Road on a grand day with traffic to pass. As you can see I´m a fan of my Harley and so long as I keep off the real dirt roads and away from the track (I don´t like being passed) the 2012-17 (I think it changed in 2017) Breakout model is my perfect bike.

author: Wayne Wilke
text: Did your 2023 Harley salesperson mention that pairing to your wireless headset (i.e. Sena) would require a WHIM - Wireless Headset Interface Module ($399) which seems to only give you ability to control volume via the motorcycle´s control? You also need to have a technician flash the infotainment $$$ in order to pair both rider and passenger headsets. Also, it won´t allow mesh comm with other bikes. author: Anthony C
text: Hello to all. I bought my 2009 FLHX in September of 2015, it had about 9,000 miles on it.   I rode it from Fayetteville, NC to Chesapeake, VA.  The first time I’ve been on a motorcycle in 13 years.   I realized after two hours of riding to my destination that I’d have to get use to riding again, because my butt was hurting.  I road it about 500 miles more and realized I had a bearing noise.   So I started with the wheel bearings.  Nope, not the problem!   I replaced the main bearing in the primary case.  Nope not it!    So I opened up the primary again, removed the chain and complete clutch pack and found the fly wheel was scuffing the inner case.   That means the main bearing in the transmission.  I pulled the transmission and replaced every bearing in it.  I also replaced the automatic primary chain adjuster with a Baker manual adjuster.  Because the automatic chain adjuster destroyed the bearings in the transmission.  I also replaced the main shaft and sized gear.  It’s bee 10,000 miles of trouble free riding!

author: Charlie
text: Black denim, screamin eagle stage one, 211 cam, rhinehart long black shotguns, mimi  apes, metezlers 888s, 50k on miles. Tried to sell it when it had 20k on it, a few years back. Listed it for 12k, not a nibble. As it was explained to me, at that time, harley wasn´t over producing, but had catch up with demand. You get all these guys that want a harley, and after 2 or 3 years of payments, and they don´t ride but a few hundred miles a year, decide it´s not worth the money spent, and put it up for sale. You can´t argue with the fact you can find 10 of the same model, with less then 4000 miles on it, what would you buy, one with 20k, or 5k or less? It ain´t the good ol days, you don´t need 10 large for a used harley. I´m glade I still got her, still a strong bike, and has served me well, with no major issues. I get to enjoy year round riding, and have gone the distance with her, a number of thou in ones, and once for kicks and giggles did 1350 in 21 hours. Drive belt is reaching its end, considering a belt primary, gotta tear it all out to get at the drive, so why not. In case your wondering, no she ain´t up for sale, and she can still bury it with both me and the ol lady saddled up.

author: will duffy
text: I own a 09 fltrse3stardust silver-platnium I now have 6.800 mi I added bassani reverse slant cut pipes and power comander, rider back rest passanger sissy bar- a detachable tour pack and a zumo 550 It runs strong but the ride is VERY hard if you hit a road strip-pot hole etc. it goes right up your back ive adjusted the shocks all that the ride sucks/im looking into new shocks. So far Ive gotten small leaks in  oil cooler (with under 4000 mi.) Motor co said it was a road hazard so I had to pay- starter and soloniod needed to be replaced the radio light stays on when you shut the bike off(Need to pull  batt. mem fuse to shut it off(both covered)but I have to wait 3 weeks for new raido)Seems they have many problems. And now I found out about the space between seat and gas tank has a bulliten on it (waiting for 2 plastic spacers for under seat. Well so much for HDs finest hand made bike Not very well thought out 4th HD never had problems like this

text: Did your 2023 Harley salesperson mention that pairing to your wireless headset (i.e. Sena) would require a WHIM - Wireless Headset Interface Module ($399) which seems to only give you ability to control volume via the motorcycle´s control? You also need to have a technician flash the infotainment $$$ in order to pair both rider and passenger headsets. Also, it won´t allow mesh comm with other bikes. Instead of pairing the Sena to the infotainment, you can just pair with your phone as usual.

Any idea as to why or are there any speaker upgrades worth buying without buying another Amp or any other accessories to power them?

author: will duffy
text: I own a 09 fltrse3stardust silver-platnium I now have 6.800 mi I added bassani reverse slant cut pipes and power comander, rider back rest passanger sissy bar- a detachable tour pack and a zumo 550 It runs strong but the ride is VERY hard if you hit a road strip-pot hole etc. it goes right up your back ive adjusted the shocks all that the ride sucks/im looking into new shocks. So far Ive gotten small leaks in  oil cooler (with under 4000 mi.) Motor co said it was a road hazard so I had to pay- starter and soloniod needed to be replaced the radio light stays on when you shut the bike off(Need to pull  batt. mem fuse to shut it off(both covered)but I have to wait 3 weeks for new raido)Seems they have many problems. Well so much for HDs finest hand made bike Not very well thought out 4th HD never had problems like this

text: Did your 2023 Harley salesperson mention that pairing to your wireless headset (i.e. Sena) would require a WHIM - Wireless Headset Interface Module ($399) which seems to only give you ability to control volume via the motorcycle´s control? You also need to have a technician flash the infotainment $$$ in order to pair both rider and passenger headsets. Also, it won´t allow mesh comm with other bikes. Instead of pairing the Sena to the infotainment, you can just pair with your phone as usual.

author: Blaine
text: Hi, Just thought I´d share a few thoughts about the CBR125 (2011 onwards) and see if anyone agrees with my opinions. I´ve recently moved to Spain from England and had to sell my Gilera SC 125. My partner is learning to ride and the CBR125 seemed like a good learner choice with reliability and linear power delivery. Pros- Very forgiving and easy driving position, linear power delivery and comfortable for twos-up riding (allowed in Spain with a car licence after two years, and no dreaded L plates needed!). Seems happy cruising at 115 kph with two; about 22 stone of passenger in total, give or take ;-) Cons- Stock tires are very poor in comparison to the Gilera (pirelli demon sport). Brakes are sub-par for a 125 ´sports´ bike. We paid 2600 euro for a 2222km 2011 bike from a honda dealer. This included Spain´s ´second hand´ vehicle tax and 1 years guarantee. I´d love to know what other owners think of the cons I mentioned. Kind regards- Blaine

author: JohnB
text: Bought new 1976 traded on a 400/4. Great looking but only did 112 to 115 (with tail wind) on the flat. Did manage 122mph  once on a mile long downhill run. Embarrassed by an old drum brake T500 Suzuki which outrun it ridden solo to 100mph and the sameT500 with a pillion passenger still flew round corners faster than my F1 Honda which grounded EVERYTHING, exhaust, centre stand and prop stand and foot-pegs too. The clutch lever had an on-off action like a trigger, very hard to slip the clutch on getting away AND if you turned the handlebars while riding the clutch pulling away from the kerbside, it suddenly dumped itself hurling you unexpectedly forward. It was totally reliable and safe to ride at speed. 100mph + speeds with your hands on your knees steering with your knees. The frame was rock solid and the trail (self straightening) was generous. I really liked it but it was uncompetitive against the 750 suzukis.

author: Robert
text: are the parts for an 05 the same for an 06 honda cbr1000?

author: Mark Brooks
text: some are but then again some are not. The 06 CBR1000RR was a mid life make over by Honda. By that i mean that the cbr normally has a 4 year life span. The keep the bike mostly the same and only tweak smaller areas. However on the 06 Honda revamped 60% of the bike. The the frame if different, the engine a lot of the engine is different. The exhaust if different. Transmission has new gears. If you know what parts you are looking for then some of them are interchangeable.Just be careful when it comes to engine parts and and make sure you check.

text: have a 90 that was difficult to start after sitting over winter.  I pulled the plug, cleaned it off, blew out the cylinder and it fired on the first kick.   Make sure your plug is clean and the chamber is dry and try it again.  Don´t over choke it.

author: Billy Conway
text: I have had a Honda CRF 50 for about 2 years. It has always ran great. It will always start easily. Most of the time only 1-2 kicks will get it to start. It will handle great on off road trails for its size. It will do about 30-36mph depending on rider weight and conditions. I would reccomend it especily if you are younger and just getting into dirt bikes and have never ridden a motorcycle before or just want a small bike to play around on. It                                           is also easy to ride because it has an automatic clutch.

author: MURRAY
text: STOCK JETTING IS 168 MAIN AND 52 PIOLOT. RUN A BOTTLE(3.5OZ) OF LUCAS 2-STROKE OIL TO 1 GALLON OF GAS AND SEE HOW FAST U STOP FOULING. ITS A SMALL 3OZ. BOTTLE AT AUTOZONE. THIS IS A 50:1 RATIO (LEAN)BUT THESE HONDA 2CYCLES RUN BETTER LEAN. IM AN MMI TECH AND USE TO RECCOMEND 32:1 BUT IT JUST DOESNT FLY WITH THESE CR´S. I LIVE IN RENO NV. AT 4,500´ ELE. I RUN A 160 MAIN AND 42 PIOLOT WITH A DG PIPE AND SILENCER AND THIS BIKE ROCKS IT UP WITH 250´S BUT IT CANT TOUCH MY YZF450. REMEMBER: IF U MAKE AN ENGINE EXHALE BETTER-U MUST MAKE IT INHALE EQUALLY AS WELL(A.FILTER) IF U GIVE AN ENGINE MORE AIR-U MUST GIVE IT MORE FUEL. AFTERMKT. PIPE KITS USUALLY COME WITH LARGER(FATTER/RICHER)JETS BC WHEN U GIVE AN ENGINE MORE AIR VOLUME U MUST GIVE IT MORE FUEL VOLUME. 50:1-40:1 THE LUCAS IS FULL SYNTHETIC AND U SHOULD ONLY USE A SYNTHETIC 2-STROKE OIL WHILE RUNNING 50:1. PEACE AND TREAD SOFT.

author: Cliff
text: I have a 2003 CRF-150 that was bored out for 175 CC displacement in 2005. All other engine components remained factory stock. Problem: The engine starts and operates very well at sea level (San Jose, CA.)I do some woodland riding at higher elevations from 3,400 ft. to 4,000 ft. above sea level. At increased altitude the engine is very difficult to start only, however operates well once running and warmed. I suspect There is a carb jet mismatch issue for the intended higher elevation operation. Is a jet replacement to a smaller size jet for leaner operation at altitude easy enough for garage mechanic like myself to acccomplish? What reduced carb jet size should I purchase from Honda inventory? Thank you, Cliff

author: Sean Renning
text: The 2014 Honda CRF250L used a Showa SFF  (Separate Function Fork) inverted 43mm front suspension setup. It uses Honda Pro Suspension Fluid SS-8 (10W) or equivalent fork oil. Fork oil level is given below, but double check with your dealer before doing anything. SHOWA SFF 43mm [right side spring, left side damping.] fork oil: Honda Pro Suspension Fluid SS-8 (10W) or equivalent fork oil level RIGHT = 122 mm fork oil level LEFT = 38 mm fork oil capacity RIGHT = 658 ml +-2.5 fork oil capacity LEFT = 683 ml +-2.5 fork oil capacity TOTAL = 1341 ml

author: Sean Renning
text: The 2014 Honda CRF250L used a Showa SFF  (Separate Function Fork) inverted 43mm front suspension setup. It uses Honda Pro Suspension Fluid SS-8 (10W) or equivalent fork oil. Fork oil level is given below, but double check with your dealer before doing anything. SHOWA SFF 43mm [right side spring, left side damping.] fork oil: Honda Pro Suspension Fluid SS-8 (10W) or equivalent fork oil level RIGHT = 122 mm fork oil level LEFT = 38 mm fork oil capacity RIGHT = 658 ml +-2.5 fork oil capacity LEFT = 683 ml +-2.5 fork oil capacity TOTAL = 1341 ml

Bike always starts and idles fine. text: Ridden a 1500 years back & at 70 years old just came across a cream puff 1800 2002 in Pewter in South Florida where I live. What was so unique about this purchase was the 2nd owners open & forthright answers to my questions. A 9 month search & this Goldwing I am buying has only 40k mileage. The owners dad gave me the serial # on the bike &called the Honda dealership to verify all service history on the motorcycle.I am buying this 70 b-day present to myself 4 months early because this deal was one that is verifiable & my personal dream come true, yes it was an obsession , one of my top 3 on my bucket list. It is going to fulfill my number 2 wish to ride from Fort Lauderdale to Chicago on it for my 50th high school reunion & birthday all in the same week.Oh yeah my #1is to plan a trip from Fort Lauderdale & ride to Seattle to meet up with my 1st and best buddy I ever met when I came to America from Ireland at 8 years old. We are going to spend the summertogether riding from Seattle to Maine, yeah coast to coast & back. Sooooo the Goldwing Cruising begins with a trailer next Wednesday to go pick up my new 2 wheeled

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

text: I just bought a 2015 Goldwing GL1800 and had it trike, by Motor Trike. It has 600 miles on it.  Gas Milage is very bad at 24 mpg. I we expecting much better. Dealer says nothing they can do, it could improve after broken in more. We have friends who have GW trikes and getting 35-40. Any suggestions for this first time GW owner?

text: Hello, I just picked up a 2010 Wing that is a level 3. It is a wrecked bike which I plan on rebuilding.  I currently own a 2005 VTX 1800F, a 1999 CBR 1100XX, and a 2005 XR 650R super moto bike. As you can probably tell, I really like my Hondas...lol. I´m in the market for several used parts to get the Wing back on the road. Please let me know if you have any suggestions. Thanks,  Chris

text: Your information doesn´t match what the owners manual says for the 2002 Goldwing.

author: JasonK
text: Last year I rented a BMW K 1600 GTL, and I toured California with this nice and big bike. I´m long legged and after riding long distances with the GTL my knees got a lot of pain. I´m now planning a similar trip, and someone is offering the Honda GoldWing as an alternative to the GTL... but reading the specs I found the GTL seat is 29,52 high and the GW seat 29,10.  So I think I would have the same problem with the GoldWing. Can anyone help me with this issue? Thank you in advance!

author: Jose
text: MIZU from Germany manufactures jack-up kits for the GoldWings, which makes the seat higher. Can look it up online )) mizushop.de/en/MIZU-jack-up-kit/3011011

author: Patrick Connelly
text: Ridden a 1500 years back & at 70 years old just came across a cream puff 1800 2002 in Pewter in South Florida where I live. What was so unique about this purchase was the 2nd owners open & forthright answers to my questions. A 9 month search & this Goldwing I am buying has only 40k mileage. The owners dad gave me the serial # on the bike &called the Honda dealership to verify all service history on the motorcycle.I am buying this 70 b-day present to myself 4 months early because this deal was one that is verifiable & my personal dream come true, yes it was an obsession , one of my top 3 on my bucket list. It is going to fulfill my number 2 wish to ride from Fort Lauderdale to Chicago on it for my 50th high school reunion & birthday all in the same week.Oh yeah my #1is to plan a trip from Fort Lauderdale & ride to Seattle to meet up with my 1st and best buddy I ever met when I came to America from Ireland at 8 years old. We are going to spend the summertogether riding from Seattle to Maine, yeah coast to coast & back. Sooooo the Goldwing Cruising begins with a trailer next Wednesday to go pick up my new 2 wheeled

author: Ken Bruce
text: This is an outstanding little mountain goat of a bike.2000 miles of use so far. tyres did 1800 miles mixed road and green laning. Deep mud and dry dirt, this bike just trots over the lot. The wheel bearings are vulnerable to liquid mud and pressure washing. Top Tips: have the saddle re padded and raised an inch or so to make it half day comfortable. Fit substantial bar risers (ive lifted them 2.5 inches). change the rear sprocket to a custom 33T. Now you can cruise at 45mph between lanes, top speed well over 70 mph which is beyond the need of most as it will ruin those soft Michelin or Dunlop trials tyres. Buy a couple of additional 1.5 Lt fuel bottles to extend the range to a decent 80+ miles. A full tank of mixed riding will do 58 miles +/-.This is simply a great piece of kit. Light, easy to ride and simply flattering to the novice. 82kg of joy.

author: John R Borges
text: I was hoping to find a reliable, unassuming dependable, low-maintenance [shaft drive] motorcycle as an apartment dweller and having the bike parked outside in open air was the reason for purchasing this beautiful, ugly bike. The all black 2019 Honda Phantom I have is unassuming, too slow and ugly for a thief to waste potential jail time for. The only things shiny are the exhaust pipes and one of them has been rusty since a month after leaving the bike shop brand new, but that´s okay, the uglier the better. Thieves are looking more for sports bikes and chrome parts; there is nothing on a Phantom worth your time or would want to haul the whole bike for if you´re a thief. The Phantom is my dream bike come true and it´s worry free, maintenance free. You can almost drive it from new to 10,000 miles before you service it, if you´re that lazy, but don´t. Follow the manual and check your oil & tires every two months, add gas and just ride. JB of Bradenton/Sarasota, FL  Motorcyclist for Jesus Christ  www.cmausa.org

author: Happy Shadow
text: I have a 1983 that runs like a top. Never have had an issue of any kind. I guess 40 years has made a difference.

author: John R Borges
text: I was hoping to find a reliable, unassuming dependable, low-maintenance [shaft drive] motorcycle as an apartment dweller and having the bike parked outside in open air was the reason for purchasing this beautiful, ugly bike. The all black 2019 Honda Phantom I have is unassuming, too slow and ugly for a thief to waste potential jail time for. The only things shiny are the exhaust pipes and one of them has been rusty since a month after leaving the bike shop brand new, but that´s okay, the uglier the better. Thieves are looking more for sports bikes and chrome parts; there is nothing on a Phantom worth your time or would want to haul the whole bike for if you´re a thief. The Phantom is my dream bike come true and it´s worry free, maintenance free. You can almost drive it from new to 10,000 miles before you service it, if you´re that lazy, but don´t. Follow the manual and check your oil & tires every two months, add gas and just ride. JB of Bradenton/Sarasota, FL  Motorcyclist for Jesus Christ  www.cmausa.org

text: Check your carburetor and air [flow] breather. I had a car with that problem when I cleaned my carburetor; ran tops at 40mph. A country mechanic told me I had put the center pin in the carb upside down. He fixed it in five minutes, for ten bucks.

author: John R Borges
text: Search your motorcycle on this same ´BIKEZ´ website and read the specifications. If it says wet-sump then your clutch is wet continuously. Check your oil level as well.  2002 ACE should be wet. Dry clutches are seldom found after 1995, or so, I believe.

author: John R Borges
text: After watching many videos on motorcycle oil test comparison, I chose synthetic Amsoil brand, which out did them all. -- However, watching other videos I noticed the oil pan removal on a car video to get to the transmission area and saw that the oil in that car did not spill, but was gloopy like

author: John R Borges
text: I would recommend OEM Honda/or Motorcycle oils. If Synthetic oil turns to Jello as I mentioned above I doubt all parts will be lubricated in an oil sump configured motorcycle engine. JB

author: Sarah Hess
text: How do you tank off the tank with fuel still in it

author: John R Borges
text: See a mechanic, or YouTube. A poor man´s fix: Siphon or empty tank. It is possible rust may not be a problem. Personally, I would put a fuel-filter to the fuel-line going to the engine. Clean the carburetor if needed. Replace the fuel-filter after a few fill ups and riding. NOTE: the fuel-filter will stop any residue and give you pure clean fuel in your engine regardless of mild tank rust. Add Marvel Mystery Oil to gas and oil tank. JB

text: im looking to buy a XR 150L in the philippines, its less than half the price of the crf 250 and i feel i get a lot of bike for the money. I am looking for engine guard, handle guard and side luggage rack. Anyone have any idea where i can buy that ?   All info recomandation concerning the XR is apreatiated. Cheers Chris

author: Charles
text: Honda Chris, I too live in the Philippines and I am on my second XR150l bought a new one after 79,000km on the first. I ride on the island of Mindanao mainly but have ridden it to most of the major islands via a ferry. I think the bike is perfect for the Philippines. I have also tried to get the same items as you and ran into the same problem, no parts. The stock handle bar seems to have a solid bar running the full length inside of it so hand guards will not work unless you replace the handlebars. Some parts are available for it in Australia but I could not find a dealer who would ship to the Philippines. I also found guys who will build parts for you but you have seen their Tricycles and quality of work. Regarding the bash plate you might try a machine shop but I suggest have a Filipino friend take it in for a quote because a foreigner always gets overcharged. Its more fun in the Philippines. I too thought about buying the CRF250L and I would have if I was going to stay the rest of my life their but I will return to the states after living their four years.

author: Cary
text: I have just purchased this bike a month ago. I moved from the states to the Philippines. I have owned a Harley since I was 16 and now I am 65. Since I have never owned a bike like this before I knew there would be a big difference between power and handling. It is OK for the roads in the Philippines. My concern is my wife and I finally took it on the srp which is like an open highway here. I noticed it really didn´t have much power and the way it is running it seems to me like it is running lean. The bike is new just out of the showroom. I looked to see where the A/F mixture value is but very difficult to get to. What is the best way to try to adjust it. I brought it to the shop about this matter but they said it is normal. I know the difference. The only way I can see to adjust the valve is to take the carburetor off. Does anyone have a better idea. Thank you

author: Jon E
text: Good day to all I would like to purchase this bike, but would like to know like tire types, tubeless or not and clutch feel. Performance overall. Thank you

author: Shortcat
text: Tyre type may depend on where you buy the bike but usually a compromise on/off road tyre (maybe 70/30). Spoke wheels always have tubed tyres. Clutch is quite good. Mine had to be run in for 1000ks which is unusual in this day & age but after 1000ks not bad for a 150cc naturally aspirated bike. Better with a few mods to improve breathing (exhaust, airbox, jetting).

Any serious off roading should bring to light any issues. I would suggest changing the tyres for something a little more suitable for off roading.

After it comes on, how many miles can you ride until it dies from empty tank?  Has the model changed much over these last few years or can I expect the same performance from a 15, 16, or 17 as I got on the 2018 demo?  Any particular plusses or minuses you´ve found on your Scout?  Thanks for any info you can provide.  My last bike was a mistake - sold it at a big loss after owning it about 3 months.

author: Sean Fenner
text: Hey everyone, I´m new to this site and desperate for help. I´ve been riding and racing motocross for over 20 years and have always done my own motor work and even built my first custom about 3 yrs ago. That being said, this KLX 110 clutch is whooping my butt. I bought the bike for a kid in the neighborhood so he doesn´t feel left out when me and my family goes to ride on weekends. The bike was barely slipping the clutches in secong and third gear with a bigger person on it. My oldest son installed some new clutch plates and springs in it last week and now it won´t move when put in gear. I wasn´t there when he first installed them but everything seems to be correct. Clutch adjustment is normal and has normal slack in cable. Side cover isn´t in a bind against pressure plate when tightening. When bike is started and put into gear you feel it go in good and solid but you let out the clutch and nothing, same in second and third gear. If you have it not running and push it and dump the clutch in gear it locks up the back wheel like it will push start.

text: What is your riding history? Are you an aggressive rider or casual? I am an aggressive woods rider and was disapointed in the KLX 300R in its origanal form. But if you are indeed using it for road also it will be the best bike for you. I am 208 lbs. I have found the suspension to be very plush. I will be going to house of horsepower to get mine set up for my style of riding though as it is to plush for fast woods and rocky areas. I feel that you will want more out of it when you get it. and trust me there is a lot more you can get out of it for fairly cheap. I bought mine got used to it and then put a full Big gun EVO R header and Exghaust with a Pumper carb from Mikuni. and found a hole nother bike. Now I am Over satisfied. I would never turn down a KLX

author: dave
text: you can´t just stab at the kickstart like a 2-stroke.  put it in nuetral, put pressure down on the kicker through two stops, after the second, go 60 degrees further, bring the kicker all the way back up, there should be a bunch of compression now and you should be at the top of the stroke, give it a quick kick all the way through the motion, it should start, if it doesn´t, start from the beginning again

author: Bear Paw
text: What is your riding history? Are you an aggressive rider or casual? I am an aggressive woods rider and was disapointed in the KLX 300R in its origanal form. But if you are indeed using it for road also it will be the best bike for you. I am 208 lbs. I have found the suspension to be very plush. I will be going to house of horsepower to get mine set up for my style of riding though as it is to plush for fast woods and rocky areas. I feel that you will want more out of it when you get it. and trust me there is a lot more you can get out of it for fairly cheap. I bought mine got used to it and then put a full Big gun EVO R header and Exghaust with a Pumper carb from Mikuni. and found a hole nother bike. Now I am Over satisfied. I would never turn down a KLX

author: dave
text: you can´t just stab at the kickstart like a 2-stroke.  put it in nuetral, put pressure down on the kicker through two stops, after the second, go 60 degrees further, bring the kicker all the way back up, there should be a bunch of compression now and you should be at the top of the stroke, give it a quick kick all the way through the motion, it should start, if it doesn´t, start from the beginning again

author: CurtC
text: I´ve had one for 2 months now (60mi). I really like it, but it does suffer from it´s CV carb. Taking out the butt plug has helped some. Just remember, no electric start.

author: Geoff
text: Hi to all , I am in the need of some technical help. Here is my problem and this is what we have checked. My problem is that the bike will not produce any power about 3/4 throttle. The bike will start to pop and miss at full throttle. Feels like ti has some sort of rev limiter device goinf on, which they do not have. So this is what we have done: Checked and tried many new spark plugs Pulled the carburetta off and cleanded twice now replaced the coil, lead and plug cap, Cleaned air filter, fuel filter in petcock replaced fuel with brand new fuel pulled th top end off to check if the rod bearing was faulty or any part of the top end was faulty check all electrical connections and metered out pins as per the book Check the time case area, by removing the cover and checking for correct timming settings. Checked all electrical wired to ensure no shorts. Check Reed Valve, and exhuast wast valve for correct operation The only thing left is the black box and the optical igition sensor. Has any one see this sort of problem before

Well My right side plastic around the turn signal is now cracked and the turn signal falls out, I have been looking everywhere (with no luck) to get the plastic replaced, well do it myself, if I could find the part. I cant find the part anywhere online, (new or used) I was wondering if anyone could help me find what I am looking for??? Thanks!

author: Andrew
text: Moved 9 months ago to a big city.  My Ninja 500 sat until today.  Got a new battery, charged it, and put it in.   Now it only stays running with the choke fully open.  Curious if anyone had any ideas on what to do.

author: brian
text: my oil smells like gas and sometimes leaks gas when i have stopped and turned of bike

author: Seriousbiker
text: The 650R doesn´t have cylinder liners, does the 500R? What is it?

author: Derek
text: I have decided I want to purchase a used Ninja 500R 2000 to 2007 model years. Are there any things I should be aware of or to watch out for in shopping for this type of bike.

author: Crystal
text: Where is a good place i can find a set of plastics for my Ninja 500r? i only really need the front end replaced, so if there is a place i can just get that, that´d be awesome too! :)

author: Ron Nilsen
text: Hi..I´m in Hackettstown NJ, and was riding 30 yrs ago...My son (21) and I bought matching Ninja 500´s to do some riding together (guess one of my last father/son things!)...So far it´s been a blast.  We both think the Ninja is excellent!  though, I plan on getting a cruiser for long trips.

author: -pE@Ce...
text: Hey guys and gals. My name is Jai and I hail from Capitol Heights, MD. A town to the east of Washington, D.C., it´s the pits lol, sike...I kid. Anyways, I have always wanted to ride and have family that do and I´ve been looking for a economical means of transportation and seeing the Ninja 500R seemed like a reasonable choice. I just wanted to join to meet some bikers and gain some insight into this world I am deciding to enter into. Laterz!

author: kawirider
text: I have a 2005 Kawi 500R and it has a hesitation when you take off.  It will fire a couple of time the catch up and take off.  However if you if you throttle up to over 4500 rpm and let off the clutch there is no hesitation.  When riding at highway speed, you can take punch it and there is not hesatation at all.  I have had the carburator looked at and the mechanic said that is was clean and working just fine.  It also has a Jardine exhaust that the owner before me put on.  He changed it from a daul exhasust to a single exhaust.  I don´t know if that could be it.  I hope some one could shed some light on this problem.

author: Goofy
text: I recently purchased a Ninja 500, the rider foot pegs are a little to high for me. Is there a Foot Peg Relocation Kit, for sale for this bike that will allow me to lower the front foot pegs at all? I think they sometimes call them Rearsets?

author: Ian
text: I bought an ´08 Ninja 500R in January of this year, and this is my very first bike.(not the first I´ve ever ridden though)It seemed like a good move back then, but now I feel like the bike is under powered. But, I´ve never ridden anything bigger to compare it to either. Should I have bought a bigger bike? Or, was this a good starter?

author: panos
text: Hello...i wanted to ask if this bike ninja500 can be found in europe...thanks

author: Rip
text: How to adjust timing chain tenshion 2005 500r ninja .seems louder then when i bought at 7.000 mi.has 13.000 now

author: tj515
text: Does anyone know of a frame slider kit for the 05

author: nick
text: does anyone know if you can get a 140/60 r17 tire on the rear of an 06 ninja 500? if so what brand is it?

author: Francis, 1, 21.
text: I just learned that the ninja 250 is not a great bike to bust wheelies. I´m pretty sure the ninja 500 is more than capable but it would b great if someone could respond that has already done it on their ninja 500, let me know.

author: twitch
text: I have an 05 500 and it is fairly easy to ride a wheelie. Just put it in second,rev it up to about 9 grand rolling about 35-40 and dump the clutch. If you have good balance and throttle control you can ride it forever.

author: Dustin
text: I was looking at the ninja 500 R for my first bike and I was wondering if it is good beginners bike?

author: Jen
text: Love this bike!  It is my first and I have been riding it for two seasons now - would not have wanted to start with any other!  It is very forgiving and an amazing bike!

author: anthony
text: i have a question to how big of a tire can I fit on a 500r it comes with a 130  i was wondering if i could go 140 150 or more does anyone know?

author: mark
text: can 500r do wheelies or what im 6´2 200... and i just put 5000 down on one just want to know

author: New Rider
text: I found a new Kawasaki Ninja 500r in my local motorcycle shop. All black, which I found out isn´t offered in 2006, but was in 2005. The sales guy quoted me the same price I have heard for 2006 bikes. What should last year´s model go for new?

author: Dommy
text: Ran out of gas the other day then switched it to reserve. Everything was fine but I am curious as to how much fuel I hace in reserve

author: luvs_the_kawi
text: I just bought a used ´02 Ninja 500R (that´s what the decals say). Is this bike equivalent to a GPZ 500 S  ?  I´m finding the naming system a bit confusing. thx Mark

author: BMan1113VR
text: They are verz similiar. The gpz is the euro/aussie version of the bike. I believe the only difference is that it has dual disks up front. everything else is the same.

author: luvs_the_kawi
text: Just got a used ´02 Ninja 500R. Man it´s nice! It´s got 6200 miles, and the rear tire will need changing soon. I envy my buddy´s 600cc Yamaha, which has a big fat rear tire. Is there a standard aftermarket tire setup for this bike that will optimize it´s handling/cornering potential? I´d like the bike to retain it´s nice all-around ride characteristics, yet I would like to feel confident when leaning hard. I would like a tire-set reccommendation that will require no alteration to the stock bike. I don´t mind sacrificing tire longevity a little bit. Also, is fatter necessarily better? What´s that fattest that I can go and still fit the stock rims, and still enable the tire to easily slip into place? Thx. Mark

author: Multilexus
text: 2003 Kawasaki Ninja 500R

author: Anupam Desai
text: Hi, just wanna know how powerful it is??? ... offcourse in BHP .....

author: Dommy
text: I belive it has around 60HP

author: duerton
text: the 500 ninja will put out around 51 horsies in the real world. but with replacement exhausts and a jet kit, you will get very close to 60hp. cheers.

author: rk
text: im considdering 2 bikes, and im wondering what the insurance and cost diference is between the two. 2005 ninja 500r and 2005 gsxr600: whats the insurance like for each and which do you think is the better value for the money.  i realize the gsxr is more, but its more bike....is it worth it?

author: mt
text: I have ridden for 4 years now, i started out on a kawa ex250, a year later the ex500 then and now, 3 years later kawa ninja z-1000 Do you know how to ride? That gsxr has 110+ hp, while the 500r has 80hp-max(swag method). The 500r is a much better starting out bike. You are more likely too pay less and less likely to eat pavement do 75 mph in 1st on the 500r.

author: BMan1113VR
text: If you are talking about a first or second bike the gixxer will kill you. GSX-Rs are the only motorcycles I know of that the manufacter speciffically turns people away from. It says on suzuki´s website that the GSX-R line up is for advanced riders only.

author: chris
text: not a bad bike, but one thing really gets me...the kickstand folds up right next to the shifter. i only wear size 10 1/2 and the folded kickstand is constantly in the way when trying to shift. after a while you get used to it, but still poor design in my opinion

author: Samuel Villalba
text: Buen dia, mi pregunta es si esta motocicleta funciona igual el sistema de frenado instalandole liquido DOT 3 o DOT 4 ??

author: Frank Hibbs
text: Is their ABS brake systems available for the Ninja 500cc sports bike or the 250cc sports bike, from Kawasaki or aftermarket ABS brake systems at a reasonable cost?  Please answer Thank you, Frank Hibbs hibbsf@yahoo.com

author: KY ASASSIN
text: Don´t usually ask for help, family first toys later. I need many parts for my 07 650R. I will take donations from anywhere I can, first rocket I´ve owned and it needs repairs. last owner droped on both sides in gravel driveway. Plastics pegs shifter brake petal and several other parts damaged. If anyone can help donate parts, I couldn´t thank you enough. Passion red, plastiics, am mechanicaly sharp, if you could mail to P.O BOX 73 petersburg ky 41080, attn Dave Sassin. Anything you can spare even if your a busisness I ride a lot and would not mind sponcering your products on my bike. If anyone has an old power commander or dyno kit it would be a dream come true. Feel free to copy this and share with anyone who could help. If I had the extra cash I would never ask. EVER needs new tires,sprockets, chain, any parts you can spare, thanks if you read this far you were drawn by someone, I only know one and that´s the good lord above. God Bless. My work cell is 859-743-9896 again David. If you ddont ask you will never receive. Bolts every little any peice you cousend or if your NKU or close by I would be glad to meetpick up or if shipping is an issue please don´t put yourself in the same position as I. Either call me, email skills0816@gmail.com thank you and any help if I can ever repay you I will. Weather its by donation of parts or if I can ever send you money, sponcer your race team, oil, local and international businesswith decals under paint I wouldn´t mind swaping plastics out weekly if I have more than enough donations. If mailing to P.O. BOX 73 ATTN: David Sassin Petersburg KY 41080. That´s a private address family members so if UPS delivers the partsPS DELIVERS I HAVE THE POST MASTER HOLDING UNTILL I MAKE IT OR MY FANILRS. DON´T FEEL SO TERRIBLE IF YOU CAN´T HELP, JUST POSTING TH

author: emmi154
text: Have an 07 650R am 4´11´/5´ . I had the bike lowered but can barely touch my toes to the ground. Are there any after market seats so I can reach the ground?

author: texas twisted
text: have you had the fork towers lowered as well? if the seat height is still too high it´ll be very hard to find a lower seat as the stock seat is extremely thin to begin with you may find a custom seat lowering kit but not sure where good luck!

author: Jharperfmx
text: Shave the seat man. Why buy a new one. Take an electric searing knife like freestyle motocross guys do and shave it to preference then stable the seat cover back on and you are golden.

author: josh
text: i had my bike in the shop for some cosmetic work last Friday.i drove it to work Monday and Tuesday. Wednesday I got to work and smelted antifreeze so I looked at the exhaust pipe coming from the motor. Around the driver side pipe one of the bolts was wet with antifreeze. Its not slow leak. I was just wondering if somebody could point me in the right direction. Thanks.

author: kevin mccarthy
text: So im new to bikes.. always had a fascination for them and a desire to learn.. but never did untill recently. i purchased a 09 650r.  it is covered in custom vynal work, some aftermarket mirrors, and underglow neons.  i recently added a fender eliminator to get rid of the rear fender.  it has from what i am told a custom, one of a kind scorpion exhaust.  i was told that they dont really make exhausts for this particular bike yet, and that this one was specially made for this bike.  idk if that is completely true or not.. but it is an awesome exhaust.  what i want to know is what next?  i know i want to get a new chain, preferrably a kaw green one, and new sprockets.. and i was also thinkin of a power commander.. what should i do first, and i was told i will have a hard time finding a power commander since the lack of exhausts made for this bike.. however i dont seem to have any trouble finding power commanders online.  just looking for some tips to make this bike that i love that much better.

author: TeXaS_TwIsTeD
text: I own the ´07 NINJA 650R & must say I LOVE IT it keeps up with or outruns the other bigger bikes has better handling & sleeker style than many of the so-called superbikes this machine is probably kaw´s greatest accomplishment up to date i´ve changed out the carbon fiber exhaust back to factory & yes it´s much quieter BUT, seems much stronger. Most people don´t understand (as i didn´t) when changing exhaust on these bikes & ALL OTHERS for that matter you MUST add the PCM \

author: Jameelea
text: My Ninja 650r is stuck in first gear...It wont go to neutral and wont shift up to any other gear.  Has anyone ever had this problem. It is a new 2009. I have only rode it 2 times.....ug

author: TeXas_TwIsTeD
text: i´ve never heard of such a problem but i´d already have it back at the dealer if i were you it´s a GREAT bike don´t give up on it :)

author: Bill
text: Well Im looking at getting my first bike. Im wanting something that I will enjoy on my own but Im also looking for a bike that I will be able to take my wife on for long rides.  Both the Ninja 650R and Honda VFR 800 I like, but Im stuck on which one will be best.  The sales guys at both dealerships have given me greats prices on both and the Ninja seams to be the best bang for my buck but Im not sure on which one is best for what Im looking for.....I guess Im saying I don´t want to make a mistake and want to have to upgrade in a few years.

author: Raven97
text: I have about 1100 miles on my ninja. It is a great bike. My biker friend needed to move my bike so he could get his out of the garage. He grabbed the aluminum handle bar at one end to move it horizontally. That movement bent the bar. Stainless steel bars would be an improvement. Worth the little extra weight. M

author: Samuel Preston I
text: I am considering purchasing a 17 zx10RR. I´ve done quite a bit of research.  I wanted to get info from current owners. What do you like most about your beloved zx10RR? Are there any issues with this I should be concerned about? What is the longevity of a engine transmission like this?  Do you recommend flashing it and if you do who is the best ecu flasher? I´m 6 3 and 260 with gear on. Well this be an issue with the electronic suspension?

author: steve pingrey
text: i have a 2007 zx10 .last week i was on a ride and my head lights started flickering .we pulled over to take a look,didn´t really see any thing we tried to start it and i would not start ,so we push started it ,and about a mile or two down the freeway it the motor stopped.i new it was my stator .the next day i replied my stator rectifier and charged my battery .iv been have trouble with my head lights for a while also.so with eve thing all put in it started up but still had trouble with the head lights ,so i unplugged my lights because i wanted to ride it ,i made tree stops and it was starting no problem.my third stop was a friends and he fixed the head lights .my four stop as i was leaving  the battery was dead would not start so we push started it again .i stopped a little way down the street to unplug my lights again be cause it was doing good when they were off.then i started on my way home and it stalled again and lost all my power ,i towed it home and now it sits in my garage and I´m as puzzled as i was 3 days before  can any one help me with my problem i don´tknow what to do next

author: steve pingrey
text: i have a 2007 zx10 .last week i was on a ride and my head lights started flickering .we pulled over to take a look,didn´t really see any thing we tried to start it and i would not start ,so we push started it ,and about a mile or two down the freeway it the motor stopped.i new it was my stator .the next day i replied my stator rectifier and charged my battery .iv been have trouble with my head lights for a while also.so with eve thing all put in it started up but still had trouble with the head lights ,so i unplugged my lights because i wanted to ride it ,i made tree stops and it was starting no problem.my third stop was a friends and he fixed the head lights .my four stop as i was leaving  the battery was dead would not start so we push started it again .i stopped a little way down the street to unplug my lights again be cause it was doing good when they were off.then i started on my way home and it stalled again and lost all my power ,i towed it home and now it sits in my garage and I´m as puzzled as i was 3 days before  can any one help me with my problem i don´tknow what to do next

author: LPW
text: I JUST BOUGHT A KAWASAKI NINJA 2007 ZX-6R.  I HAVE NO OWNERS MANUAL YET AND I KNOW NOTHING ABOUT THIS BIKE.  I AM VERY INTERESTED IN WHAT MAKES THIS BIKE DIFFERENT FROM THE OTHER 2007 KAWA NINJA´S.  I WENT TO BERT´S BIKE SHOP AND ONE 2007 USED NINJA SOLD FOR $11,000 AND LOOKED JUST LIKE ANOTHER 2007 NINJA WHICH SOLD FOR $8,000.  NO ONE AT THE DEALER COULD TELL ME WHY ONE BIKE COST MORE THAT THE OTHER.  THE SAME WITH THE NEW BIKES - A 2011 NINJA COULD SELL FOR $7,999 AND ANOTHER 2011 NINJA WOULD SELL FOR $15,000.  I KNOW ONE BIKE IS BETTER THAN THE OTHER, BUT NO SALEMAN COULD TELL ME WHAT WAS DIFFERENT.  I ONCE WAS A MECHANIC AND I UNDERSTAND, CAMS, FUEL INJECTION, BETTER SHOCKS, BETTER FRAME AND ALL THAT STUFF,  SO CAN SOME ONE TELL ME WHAT IS THE DIFFERENCE. author: Gord Klippenstei
text: The versys has been commented on throughout the web, and I can only do so on my own experiences riding this bike. I own a 2007 model which has 8000 kil. One trip I made was 4900 kil in 5 days with no problems.It cruises at 90 to 100 mph if you prefer but watch out for tickets. The motor is not overstressed and delivers good gas mileage. I got over 300 kil to a tank. I found this 400 lb bike super stable in heavy winds, more so than some heavier bikes, maybe due to the design of small fairing. The windshield was moved to highest position, and some riders would prefer a taller model which I will install. There are side bags available, but right now I use a 3 in one rear rack by Venture which has held a lot of weight, and thought it was going to break, but not a problem and was really impressed for you can place luggage bag forward or rearward in seconds or use two of them. The front one does,nt even touch the seat which is a plus. Handling of bike is great, even when I rode through 300 miles of rain and downpour.Sevicing this bike is a breeze,like quick oil changes, etc. The chain makes no noise what so ever and did,nt need adjustment yet. The seat is as comfortable as most, but for long trips, I utilize a sheepskin cover which makes it that more comfortable. I have cruise control and gps installed which I would,nt go without. The vibrations of this bike are counteracted by the engine balancer and are acceptable when I ride it at any speed. I believe the bike will do 128 mph which is plenty fast for me, but have,nt pushed it that far. It has a sump plate at bottom of engine and a casette transmission for ease of any maintenance or repair. Warranty is 1 year and can be extended to 3 or 4.I did have a buzzing at fairing, but corrected it. Contact me if I can share more info.

author: Karen
text: My bike´s seat is stuck.  I turn the key and I can see it pulling the cable, but the seat doesn´t pop up like usual.  I need to charge my battery, but can´t do that until I can figure out how to get the seat off.  (I have a 2006 Vulcan 900 Classic - no modifications yet). Any suggestions? Thanks!

author: Bigdaddyrkt
text: Karen, Try to press down near the front center of the passenger seat.  The latch is just below that area.  Mine did that, even worse I had saddlebags under the seat which made the latch bind even worse causing me to bend the key.  You can remove the male end of the latch under the seat and add a few washers under the bolts allowing less binding at the latch. You could also place some washers under the bolts of the female latch release.  Either way the binding is what makes the latch not release easy. Hope this helps.

author: dm
text: I have owned this bike since May 09, and it is a great bike to say the least. It is about $4K cheaper than the HD Electra Glide Ultra Classic (which comes stock with a smaller engine!), and that only adds to the pleasure. Handling is great, comfort is great, power is great (cruises at highway speed under 3K rpm). It has everything I would normally consider adding to a stock bike, so no need for tons of accessories. The bike does not come with as much audio equipment as either a HD or a Goldwing, but the price of the bike reflects this. Overall a solid bike and a good value.

author: Dillitype
text: Ain´t this a great era to ride bike in! I have had most all Jap brands of bikes AND two Harleys.  All bikes are better than they were 40 years ago when I started my two-wheeled motorized adventures.  At the end of a long ride it doesn´t matter what label of bike got you to your destination, the beer will taste just as good and your woman will feel just as nice.  The fact that I have survived long enough on two wheels (started 1967) to now enjoy traveling accross this great land of ours on any brand of motorcycle with little fear of breakdown or instrument failure is rewarding to me.  This new Kawi looks good to me and I hope it is successful.  The equipment offered to riders today is all high quality and I´m glad to be part of the ride. I want to share a story, because bikes aren´t the only thing evolving in todays motorcycle world.  The people themselves are changing too. One guy I met a few years ago looked like a grizzled biker when he approached me.  I complemented him on his nice looking sporty and he commented, \

text: I own a 2006 VN900D (not in California) with 16,000 miles on it.  An intermittent noise has started which seems to emanate from the lower left side of the engine. Has anyone experienced this noise with your Vulcan? Please clue me in if you have. Thanks, Randy in St. Louis

author: Karen
text: My bike´s seat is stuck.  I turn the key and I can see it pulling the cable, but the seat doesn´t pop up like usual. author: Bigdaddyrkt
text: Karen, Try to press down near the front center of the passenger seat.  The latch is just below that area.  Mine did that, even worse I had saddlebags under the seat which made the latch bind even worse causing me to bend the key.  You can remove the male end of the latch under the seat and add a few washers under the bolts allowing less binding at the latch. You could also place some washers under the bolts of the female latch release.  Either way the binding is what makes the latch not release easy. Hope this helps.

author: Lets Ride
text: Ok. Here is the deal. I have been in the market for some time for a new bike. I had Honda ACE which I loved. It was time to modernize and take the plunge. Test rode the Shadow 1100 and could not help but wonder why Honda is still pumping out old technology but then again its sales records keep it going strong. It felt very similar to my ACE in regards to overall torque. Went down the list on the big 4 and had to keep going back to the 900 Custom. Not only does the bike look so dam awesome close up, it has a tremendous presence when riding. Great torque and it handles fantastic. So I bought it. Have 1340 miles on it now and it just keeps getting better! The bike looks so huge that no one can believe it is a 900. And as far as speed I would put it up against any 1100 Cruiser on the market. In addition to great power it has silky smooth shifting and outstanding handling. All of this for 7300 bucks? It is about time! There is NOTHING in its price range that could come close! Thank you Kawasaki!

author: IAMDPREDATOR
text: When the Z1R was introduced in 1978, the fastest thing on the road was the Kaw 750 2-stroke. I pulled away from one the first week I got my Z1R. I raced every kind of bike and car for a couple of years without a loss. The summer of 1980 a GS1100 showed me a loss. I still have a Z1R today, having bought and sold several over the years. Mine now is a mix of the 78 and 80 models. The best of the mix. I built a engine and beat that Suzuki that handed me my first loss btw, the high speed wobble got me and I pulled that engine and it is still sitting in my garage. Kawasaki made us gods in our own right back in the day. Now the ZX-14 has ruled the streets and this year the Super charged 1000 looks to take that over. KAWASAKI RULES.

author: Bob
text: Chicago?  I´m a couple hours south of you brother!  Nice.... Good luck finding a disk.  I´m sure they are out there.  I found an old manual that has greasy finger print markings on it! My KZ 1000 LTD is stock and no mods.  When I got her, the 4th owner with history, I´ve restored it to original; replacing the seat, side pods with correct markings, factory tires and grab rail for the seat. No bags on mine and honestly?  I don´t know where to begin to look for them.  In fact, I would doubt in 1982 that they were even an option back then.  I would try the factory first, only to see it they were available and if so, then certainly the aftermarket folks may have followed suit.  Hardware may be difficult and then to mount so you may have to customize. Handle bars? Hmmmmm.  I´d hate to advise the ‘trail and error’ approach as that´s money and your labor time.  But I would find what ´looks´ to be what you want, and then take measurements for all your cables; throttle, brake, clutch as you may have to make adjustments there relative to length.  As far as mounting, that shouldn’t be an issue as long as the mounting of the bar is like for like – make sense? Sorry I couldn´t be much more help.  Good luck and next time I head North, I´ll let you know.  Later, Bob....

author: Bob
text: I’m the 4th owner of this 32-year old ride with 24,044 original miles and have all the history [having sat 20-years during a divorce settlement with owner #2].  Anyway; I have restored this to the way it sat on the showroom in 1982 and everything works…. Except one thing that has been kicking my half-moons.  The automatic turn signal cancellation feature with this bike.  I went on line and found two (2) control modules for this…. no luck.  Then?  Thinking it may be the contacts on the left handle bar turn signal control button itself, I bought a new one for almost $250.  I installed it, took the bike out and?  BINGO!  Fixed!  Worked like a charm [note I didn’t say ‘works’…. Rather ‘worked’]. I’d say after no less than 500 miles, during a rain storm, racing to get back home, while coming off the interstate, it went on, but didn’t ‘auto’ cancel to off.  It has been that way for over a year now as I struggle to find the why behind that. For you smart folks out there; my speedo works and tracks both overall miles and the trip odometer works too that from the Tech Manual, is part of this ‘circuitry’ as best I can tell, feeding into this control unit that is suppose to cancel.  BOTH turn signals work, come on manually and blink, but I have to manually switch them back off or I’m like the old man on the interstate with a blinker left on. Any insights, similar experience or suggestions and feedback is needed and I would greatly appreciate.  Once this is resolved, everything will be working on this 32-year old puppy.  Thanks in advance for any help you can offer and if you are in the area, attending the Springfield Mile Motorcycle races tomorrow, look me up and I’ll buy you a beer.  Happy Memorial Day everyone.  Bob….

text: I had that bike new in 77................it was called a KZ400 Special.......it was a barebones bike advertised at $999.95 No tach, no chrome fenders. 2-into 1. There was an engine design flaw and they leaked oil at the head on the left side. Re-engineered next as a 440 with an external oil line to fix the problem. you could ride for ever with just a quick rag wipe and an eventual top off on oil. Bike was quick fo a 400. Rated Top speed of 102mph Ive done 110 mnph. Light frame very tossable. Nice torque could pull a slight 2nd gear wheelie if you tried. Was faster than my buddies Honda  CB400F and anothers Yamaha RD400. The Yamaha ran out of steam in 3rd but ripped ahead until then. It was a 2 stroke. The biggest drawback was it came in only one color..it was a pinkish red..... more pink than red

author: Affen
text: If you have a spark and the engine won´t start then you 99% sure have no fuel in the cumbustion chamber. The remaining 1% could be due to lack of compression or a short circuit of the ignition loop after you have installed the plug. To make any engin start, I have found that pouring one shot glass of petrol into the air filter always works, if everything else is working. If it stops, try again and then if it stops, check the fuel flow, because it is 100% to be discontinued somewhere between the tank and the cylinders.

author: NN
text: When I was looking for some early 1980s used smaller bike, I came across several of those inevitable 400cc Japanese twins of that era. The Suzuki GS400E might be faster (fastest 27 din hp bike in those days - I got my driving licence on one of those), but the Z 400 is more of a beauty to me. When I was surfing the Internet for some details on the Z 400, I found that the model wasn´t quite popular when new. Too slow, not enough Kawasaki (i.e. small frame, much power, bad handling, etc..). In spite of those warnings I got myself that beatiful 1981 model (low miles, not rotten at all) I fell in love with at first sight. Alternatives had been, of course the Yamaha 500 SR, some Suzukin 550 Katana and a Honda 500 CBR. Now I own the J Custom model, black with gold-linings. The bike runs considerably slower than specified (top 140km/h) and it not easy to get some of the acessories of the day, but spare-parts isn´t that much of a problem. I never regretted buying this one: - looks good (at least that "Custom"-model - rides ok, if you´re not into that sports thing - is reliable (YES, MINE IS - IN EVERY RESPECT) - economy is alright, too So if you´re up to get yourself one of those check out: -) there´s a really a LOT of different models in the 400 Z series, some look like sports. Something they´re not, of course. With a bike performing like mine you´ll be disappointed. -) There´s word that fine-tuning the carbs is quite of a problem. Be sure that you can handle the problem or know (or pay) someone who can. Because I like that little bike I treat it like a new one and let it have its routine insepections (just like the guy who owned it before) -) Of course just of age alone: Chrome, screws and stuff is prone to rust, you better take care of that -) It´s a nice bike for everyday riding and a nice weekend about the country. Cruising and stuff. -) It´s no Autobahn-Racer, it´s not first choice for agressive cornering (not even about backwater roads)

My bike has painted front and rear fenders, an original (Kawasaki etched in the pipe) 2-1 exhaust and only has a speedometer, NO tach. - Is this a hard to find bike? Model (VIN states K4-#####)?

author: jd111358
text: I had that bike new in 77................it was called a KZ400 Special.......it was a barebones bike advertised at $999.95 No tach, no chrome fenders. 2-into 1. There was an engine design flaw and they leaked oil at the head on the left side. Re-engineered next as a 440 with an external oil line to fix the problem. you could ride for ever with just a quick rag wipe and an eventual top off on oil. Bike was quick fo a 400. Rated Top speed of 102mph Ive done 110 mnph. Light frame very tossable. Nice torque could pull a slight 2nd gear wheelie if you tried. Was faster than my buddies Honda  CB400F and anothers Yamaha RD400. The Yamaha ran out of steam in 3rd but ripped ahead until then. It was a 2 stroke. The biggest drawback was it came in only one color..it was a pinkish red..... more pink than red

author: Help!
text: I´ve rode dirt bikes and ATVS for a few years when I was younger but this is my first motorcycle. I have a 1976 kz400 an it runs great or the most part. Starts first kick with no choke and ideals great at first, but after I take it around the block and stop it won´t idle I have to keep on the as a little or it will cut off. When it does cut off it starts right back up. I read online that the early models of this bike are prone to idleing issues. Before I jump in head first ad start to rebuild the carbs anyone have any suggestions for a quick fix? Or maybe some things I should check? I would greatly appreciate it.

author: Affen
text: If you have a spark and the engine won´t start then you 99% sure have no fuel in the cumbustion chamber. The remaining 1% could be due to lack of compression or a short circuit of the ignition loop after you have installed the plug. To make any engin start, I have found that pouring one shot glass of petrol into the air filter always works, if everything else is working. If it stops, try again and then if it stops, check the fuel flow, because it is 100% to be discontinued somewhere between the tank and the cylinders.

author: NN
text: 1980 Z400 twin 4,500 miles £450. I saw the advert and knew that this was the commuting bike for me.I had a look at it,and yes in was in very good condition ( unused in a carport for 14 years) and owned for a year by a "born again biker".That was a year ago,it has been ridden almost everyday and it has never let me down.It now has 12,750 on the clock (I´ve increased the milage of a 23 year bike 3 times over in a year!).The only fault is a constant supply of headlight bulbs due to the "white finger" inducing vibrations. Its not a speed machine but it beats travelling by car to work everyday.

author: NN
text: When I was looking for some early 1980s used smaller bike, I came across several of those inevitable 400cc Japanese twins of that era. The Suzuki GS400E might be faster (fastest 27 din hp bike in those days - I got my driving licence on one of those), but the Z 400 is more of a beauty to me. When I was surfing the Internet for some details on the Z 400, I found that the model wasn´t quite popular when new. Too slow, not enough Kawasaki (i.e. small frame, much power, bad handling, etc..). In spite of those warnings I got myself that beatiful 1981 model (low miles, not rotten at all) I fell in love with at first sight. Alternatives had been, of course the Yamaha 500 SR, some Suzukin 550 Katana and a Honda 500 CBR. Now I own the J Custom model, black with gold-linings. The bike runs considerably slower than specified (top 140km/h) and it not easy to get some of the acessories of the day, but spare-parts isn´t that much of a problem. I never regretted buying this one: - looks good (at least that "Custom"-model - rides ok, if you´re not into that sports thing - is reliable (YES, MINE IS - IN EVERY RESPECT) - economy is alright, too So if you´re up to get yourself one of those check out: -) there´s a really a LOT of different models in the 400 Z series, some look like sports. Something they´re not, of course. With a bike performing like mine you´ll be disappointed. -) There´s word that fine-tuning the carbs is quite of a problem. Be sure that you can handle the problem or know (or pay) someone who can. Because I like that little bike I treat it like a new one and let it have its routine insepections (just like the guy who owned it before) -) Of course just of age alone: Chrome, screws and stuff is prone to rust, you better take care of that -) It´s a nice bike for everyday riding and a nice weekend about the country. Cruising and stuff. -) It´s no Autobahn-Racer, it´s not first choice for agressive cornering (not even about backwater roads)

author: Casey
text: I would greatly appreciate some help figuring out why my bike won´t start. I have this same kind of bike. I´m getting power to the coil but there´s no power going to the spark plugs. So the spark plugs are not firing. Is there something I haven´t checked other than the cylinoid and the ground? Thanks for your time.

author: Leo
text: I have had this same problem many times in the past with mine.  There are a couple of things that have caused it over the years though.  The clutch cuttoff was one cause.  The switch in the clutch lever to prevent starting until the clutch was depressed went bad and I ended up cutting out the switch all together instead of replacing it.  Another problem is that the wires from the coils switch colors at one of the connectors.  This is a strange one but my wiring diagram proved it when I got to sit down and read it.  The biggest cause for me and that problem though is the fuse box.  The connectors for the fuses started to corode a little and while it showed I had power and should run it was not enough to turn it over or fire it.  Try these out and tell me if they help.

text: Could be.  Depends on which gasket needs to be replaced. Think of it this way though, the bike really isn´t worth that much besides sentimentally so if it is something serious it is probably much more serious so your gonna need to drop some cash.  If I were you I would replace the gasket and put my faith in Kawasaki engineering.

author: MikeS
text: Does anyone have knowledge of a ´78 z650 with dual disc brakes up front? I have the spoke wheels front and back. Front brake is dual disc on spoke wheels. The rear brake is the expanding type. I was told the front end (forks, wheel, brake) is original as is when bought. Also, anyone with info on master cylinder rebuild or new kits would be appreciated.  Thanks!

author: rstnick
text: The spoked models (B1-4) have a single disk up front and drum brake in the rear. The C models, with mag wheels have two disks up front and a disk in the back. Check out kzrider.com for lots of great info. Rob.

author: seventex

From what I can see, the differences between the KZ and the Z are cosmetic... beside the dual disks on the front, wheels, the saddle and exhaust seem a little different.

author: Cal
text: My issue....the high beam indicator lamp on the instrument cluster will become dimly lit with the headlight on low beam (at idle) and become brighter as the RPMs increase. The high/low switch works and will act as it should along with the high beam indicator and headlight. What´s really weird tho...as I ride with the low beam on, the H/B indicator lamp continually gets brighter until I use a turn signal, then it goes dim again. It´s like I´m building capacitance somewhere in the system? I have the US and European schematics and went as far as completely removing all wiring (again) and checked it with a DMM then rechecked it with everything mounted and powered up. I can´t find anything wrong? Anyone have any ideas? (I used LED replacements in the cluster but it does the same thing with OE bulbs so I´m at a loss) Thanks for any ideas you send my way!!! Ride Safe!

author: ByronBrown
text: Introduction: I have owned 3 bikes, a Yamaha 125 twin, a Kawasaki 175 trail and the incomparable Z1. I bought the newly arrived Z1 on impulse and never regretted it. A friend, Peter, (I recently helped him get his M/C licence), invited me to the showrooms. For the test drive we were bluntly told if you drop it you buy it so Peter gave me the nod. I had an immediate affinity with the big bike. I loved the turbine whine, reminded me of the jumbo jet take offs. Confident to command, comfortable to travel and later I discovered the awesome stopping power during an altercation with a drunk driver. The Z1 out stopped, by a large margin, a 4 wheel locked-up squealing large sedan travelling in the same direction. It was the first time I saw the front forks travel, yawning the frame into a we mean business stop. Back to the test drive and I chucked a very reluctant Peter off the bike to see the difference and saw none. Skinny Pete then had a go, we returned and the surprised vendor made 2 sales. News of my then top of the line bike purchase quickly got around. Now this soon happened twice within a few days. I was asked, by two different people, why 900cc? A 250 or maybe 300 tops would have stacks of power and on both occasions I felt like a gasping fish out of water, caught me off guard, didn´t make me feel good at all, shook my tree a bit. A few days later I realized the answer. Look directly at the guy with a big genuine smile and say "Have you ridden one?" and boy was I ready for number three. A great bike, possibly one of the greatest of its time in my limited view as I would be hard pressed to suggest improvements. On thing though, the exhaust system had started to rust along a weld seam after one year. Perhaps stainless steel next time? Thank you for the opportunity to contribute to this forum. Byron Brown.

author: Garry
text: My parents bought me one for me passing and getting out of high school...hahhahah I kept it for 2yrs then bought a 1977 1000 LTD.  I enjoyed the LTD but really missed the 900.  Well guess what, now I am looking to buy one 30 yrs later after high school.  It was one of the best bike´s built in the 70´s and is still one of the best bike´s.  Iam glad you are about to enjoy yours.  Good riding.

author: PHIL Capano
text: i bought mt z1 the week between christmas and new years 1974-1975.its color was blue,i had three to choose from 2 maroon and one blue..i traded a cb550 for the bike.the bikes price was $2750.00 brand new.it was incredibly fast.. i put denco cams,air pods and a kerker header on it, rejetted the carbs,i ran A/ss thats A super stock at the local dragstrip.it ran 11.90s at around 105 mph all day..had it painted all black with the chrome engine kit it was a stunning machine,should have kept it..sold it in 1980 for $1800.00.best bike i have ever owned and that counts 3 hondas and 2 harleys..this thing ate sportsters with ease..thanks for reading..
